sections.paragraph:
ἐνυβρίζω 1 aor. ἐνύβρισα (s. ὑβρίζω; Soph. et al.; Polyb.; Diod S; Kaibel 195, 1; POxy 237 VI, 17; TestSol 4, 2 D; JosAs 23:4 [ἐνυβρίσθην]; Jos., Ant. 20, 117 al.; Ath. 2, 2 εἰς τὸ ὄνομα; 24, 4 τῇ ἀρχῇ) to insult, outrage τὶ (Jos., Ant. 1, 47 μου τὴν γνώμην=God’s command) τὸ πνεῦμα Hb 10:29.—DELG s.v. ὕβρις. M-M. TW.

aor. aor. = aorist

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Kaibel Kaibel = Epigrammata Graeca ex Lapidibus Conlecta, ed. GK.—List 3

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 342
